analysis_description: Round 3 tape days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol from voucher mid and VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT wall mid; natural cubic spline IV vs log(K); leave-one-out IV prediction RMSE; best bid/ask spread stats; correlation of Δ(underlying wall mid) vs Δ(IV at strike nearest S) across subsampled timestamps. TTE: from round3work/round3description.txt, tape CSV day index d (0,1,2 in prices_round_3_day_d.csv) maps to TTE = (8−d) days; year fraction T = TTE_days/365.25. BS: r=0, no dividend.
used_to_decide: Confirmed spline interpolates observed IV smile tightly in-sample; LOO RMSE ~0.75–1.37 vol pts shows cross-strike interpolation noise (worse on day 0). Spread median 2 with mean ~5 suggests penny strikes need tighter absolute edges than deep ITM. Weak positive corr(ΔS, ΔIV_atm) on later tape days steered light extract quote skew vs voucher delta in the trader.

analysis_description: Per-strike spline-edge + Greek analysis on ROUND_3 days 0-2. For each timestamp: fit robust cubic spline IV(logK) from BS implied vols (S from VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT wall mid; TTE = 8-d days from round3description). Compute fair price via BS(spline IV), buy_edge=fair-ask1, sell_edge=bid1-fair, vega, and vega*iv_error. Summarize hit-rates and edge magnitudes by strike.
used_to_decide: Observed persistent positive edge primarily in VEV_4000 (buy_hit_rate ~0.004 with very large edge when present), while other strikes showed near-zero/negative top-of-book edge after spread in this backtester. This motivated v3/v4 to keep the same spline-IV thesis but add edge-gated multi-level taking and faster inventory recycling, with extra aggression only for VEV_4000.

analysis_description: Design note for v5/v6 (no new CSV): after v3/v4 underperformed v1, parent was reset to best PnL trader_v1. Hypothesis: BS vega weights how much 1 tick of mid move in price space is worth in IV space; combine with observed bid-ask width (spread ticks) to modulate required edge—tighter edge when |vega| is large and spread is narrow. Small grid: VEGA_EDGE_SCALE 0.15 (v5) vs 0.25 (v6). TTE unchanged: 8−d days per round3description.
used_to_decide: v5 and v6 beat v1 in this backtest (50,261 vs 50,222). v5 and v6 tie on PnL; prefer v5 (smaller scale) per tie-break. v3/v4 multi-level aggressor path still recorded as worse than v1 here, so not pursued further without new evidence.

analysis_description: Mean |BS vega| and |BS gamma| (using spline implied vol at each strike) by voucher and tape day, subsampled 1/500. Confirms |γ| and |vega| peak near 5200–5300; deep ITM (4000) has very different Greek profile than OTM. Motivates optional gamma-caution in edge (v7/v8); in this backtest, gamma scaling 100 vs 160 did not change total PnL vs v5.
used_to_decide: Kept v5 as the leading branch; v7/v8 are documented experiments—same 50,261 aggregate PnL, so the simpler v5 (no extra gamma) remains the preferred tie-break v5 family member unless future runs diverge.

analysis_description: LOO vs in-sample spline fair (BS price from spline IV at each strike): subsample 1/500, same robust global outlier drop as traders, then for each strike compare |BS(S,K,T,IV_LOO) - BS(S,K,T,IV_in)| with LOO omitting that strike's node. Deep ITM VEV_4000 shows largest mean absolute fair gap on day 0 (~62 seashells in this sample), motivating leave-one-out fair for trading that strike while other strikes have small gaps.
used_to_decide: Implemented trader_v9 = trader_v5 logic but fair IV at K from leave-one-out spline when five or more nodes (else in-sample). Backtest total 67,649 vs 50,261 for v5 on the same regime—new best; v5/v6 family superseded for this dataset run.

analysis_description: Using prior strike-edge/vega_iv_err concentration from analysis_outputs/strike_edge_greeks_detailed.csv, edge signal remains concentrated in VEV_4000 while several other strikes have weaker/less stable edge despite active trading under v9. Added a risk-shaping experiment: keep leave-one-out spline fair, but taper order sizes as option delta-utilization grows (symbol-level |position * delta| proxy), stronger taper for non-4000 strikes. Parameter grid: DELTA_HEADROOM 0.35 (v10) vs 0.50 (v11).
used_to_decide: v11 outperformed v9 (68,908 vs 67,649), while v10 was slightly below v9 (67,882). Looser headroom (0.50) retains enough flow in productive strikes while dampening unstable non-4000 inventory build-up.

analysis_description: Extension of the v10/v11 delta-headroom grid: DELTA_HEADROOM 0.55 (v12) and 0.65 (v13) on the same LOO spline + vega-spread edge + delta-dependent size taper as v11. No new CSV; backtest-only parameter sweep.
used_to_decide: Both v12 (68,479) and v13 (68,566) trail v11 (68,908). Suggests the 0.50 headroom is near-optimal on this tape; higher headroom keeps size up but does not improve total PnL here. Keep v11 as the best branch for this family.

analysis_description: Underlying propagation deep-dive: contract-level response of voucher mids to extract mid moves (contemporaneous and lag-1 beta/correlation), plus spread behavior under |dExtract| shock regimes (95th percentile by day). Files: underlying_propagation_by_strike.csv and underlying_propagation_shock_regimes.csv. Combined with existing IV/Greek thread (LOO spline fair, vega/delta controls).
used_to_decide: Findings: voucher response is mostly contemporaneous (lag-1 beta ~0), beta declines by strike (e.g., ~0.74 at VEV_4000 to near 0 at 6000/6500), and spreads widen only mildly during large extract shocks. Implemented shock-aware edge widening in v14/v15; both matched v11 total PnL (68,908), implying little incremental benefit in this dataset. Keep v11 as preferred simpler tie-break.

analysis_description: Up vs down extract moves: on |dExtract|>0.5 steps, separate OLS beta of dVoucher on dExtract for up days vs down days, plus mean spread on those steps. Script: analyze_extract_updown_asymmetry.py; extract_updown_asymmetry.csv.
used_to_decide: For several strikes (e.g. VEV_4000–5200) beta_down was >= beta_up on large steps, suggesting mild asymmetry; spreads on up vs down steps were nearly identical. Implemented v16 = v11 + momentum shift to fair (K MOMENTUM_K * sum(dS) * BS delta) to lean with extract trend in a delta-consistent way; backtest 67,116 < v11 68,908. Falsified extra fair nudge—revert to v11 for this dataset unless a different mapping is justified.

analysis_description: Residual alpha test from extract propagation: build residual r_t = dVoucher_t - beta*dExtract_t (beta from same-day fit per strike), then test corr(r_t, dVoucher_{t+1}). residual_alpha.csv showed broad negative correlation (mean reversion) for active strikes, including tight-spread subsets for several strikes.
used_to_decide: Implemented v17/v18 fair correction using previous residual. Despite apparent signal in tape, backtests degraded materially (61,236 and 29,560 vs 68,908 v11), likely due to execution friction and overreaction in mid strikes (VEV_5000/5100). Conclude this residual correction is not robust under worse-fill regime; keep v11 as leading variant.

analysis_description: Top-of-book depth by strike: from prices_round_3_day_0-2, for each VEV row compute min(bid_volume_1, |ask_volume_1|) and spread. Pooled mean min-top ~16.4 (median 16) across 300k rows; strike structure: VEV_4000/4500/5000/5100 have thinner min-top (~9-15 mean) and wider spreads, while 5200-6000 have min-top ~19-22 and 1-3 tick spreads. 6500: min-top ~15, tight spread. Script: analyze_vev_book_depth_by_strike.py; vev_book_depth_by_strike.csv.
used_to_decide: Hypothesis: after v11 delta taper, further scale MM/TAKE by (min(REF, min_top)/REF)^BETA with REF=16 from pooled tape. v19 (BETA=0.5) total 68,054; v20 (0.25) 68,433 vs v11 68,908. Both trail v11, so do not replace LOO+vega+delta sizing with this depth path; the fixed baseline sizes are already in-range vs typical depth and the extra shrink hurts fill quality under worse-matching. Keep v11 as best.

analysis_description: Directional propagation asymmetry integration test. From extract_updown_asymmetry.csv and prior notes: for active strikes, beta_down >= beta_up on large |dExtract| steps while spreads are near-symmetric by sign. Kept IV/Greek core unchanged (LOO spline IV fair + vega-spread edge + delta taper) and tested directional asymmetry only in take thresholds using current extract move sign.
used_to_decide: Implemented v21/v22 from v11 with take-edge asymmetry: after down extract step, require more buy-call edge and less sell-call edge; reverse on up steps. Parameter sweep ASYM_EDGE_K=0.20 (v21) and 0.35 (v22). Both backtests tied v11 exactly at 68,908 total under worse fills, with identical day totals. No measurable gain from added complexity; keep v11 as preferred tie-break.

analysis_description: Read `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt` (merged from main): joint gate when VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 both have BBO spread (ask1-bid1) ≤ 2; forward extract mid (K=20) higher when both tight; r3_tight_spread_summary (day 0) P(tight)≈0.10, mean(FWD)|tight ≈ 0.55 vs ≈-0.07 not-tight, t-stat ≈4.7. Spreads: corr(s5200,s5300)≈0.22. STRATEGY.txt stresses mid ≠ PnL after spread and worse-matching.
used_to_decide: trader_v23 = v11 + mild gate: tight → +5% VEV sizes, take_edge mult 0.98, extract skew 0.03; wide → 0.95/1.04/0.02. HYDRO restated like v11. Worse: total 68,304 over 4 backtester days; all: 70,332. Slightly below v11 on comparable head-to-head; joint gate as implemented does not help yet. Next: try gate that only modulates EXTRACT, or only size when both tight, with smaller deltas.
